title: NOC 70021 – Postal and courier services managers | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Postal and Courier Services Managers (NOC 70021) are the operational anchors of Canada’s booming e-commerce economy. As the Courier, Express, and Parcel (CEP) market in Canada is projected to reach $16.10 billion this year, these managers are overseeing the transition to high-speed automated sorting and "green" last-mile delivery fleets. Operating at the TEER 0 (Management) level, they direct the movement of millions of parcels through the networks of Canada Post and private giants like FedEx, UPS, and DHL.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category0 (Management occupations)Major Group70 – Middle management occupations in trades and transportationSub-Major Group700 – Middle management occupations in trades and transportation

duties: Operational Leadership: Planning, organizing, and directing the activities of postal facilities and courier companies or their internal divisions.
Sorting Efficiency: Managing the accurate and expedient movement of mail and parcels through high-volume sorting facilities.
Process Improvement: Reviewing the effectiveness of operations and implementing technological or structural changes to meet 2026 delivery speeds.
Fiscal Management: Preparing and submitting budget estimates and administering the expenditures of sorting plants and stations.
HR Oversight: Interviewing, hiring, and providing specialized training for supervisory and operational staff.
employment_requirements: Education: A university degree or college diploma is usually required.
Experience: Several years of supervisory or operational experience within a postal or courier facility are mandatory.
Skills: High proficiency in data analysis, resource management, and conflict resolution is expected for 2026 management roles.
Regulation: This occupation is not regulated in Canada, meaning no professional license is required to practice, though internal corporate certifications are common.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Balanced, with demand fueled by the "sub-24-hour fulfillment" commitments of major retailers:
Ontario (GTA): Very Good prospects. As the logistics hub of Canada, the Greater Toronto Area has the highest density of sorting facilities and regional distribution centers.
British Columbia: Strong demand. Driven by the "Pacific Gateway" international parcel inflows and a high median wage of $45.96/hr.
Alberta: Good prospects. Demand is concentrated in the Calgary-Edmonton corridor, supporting the rapid growth of direct-to-consumer (D2C) shipping models.
National Trend: Over the 2024–2033 period, Canada expects roughly 1,400 job openings in this sector, primarily driven by the retirement of the 38% of workers currently aged 50 or older.
Why Work in Canada as a Postal/Courier Manager?
E-commerce Backbone: Lead the "Logistics Revolution" as online retail continues to grow at a 4.62% annual rate through 2031.
Technological Integration: Manage 2026-grade infrastructure, including automated parcel-locker networks and AI-driven route optimization.
High-Tier Earnings: With a national median salary of approximately $96,000, this management role offers long-term financial stability.
New Immigration Priority: For the first time, "Transport Occupations" and "Managers with Canadian Experience" are highlighted as core priority categories for 2026 Express Entry draws.
pathways: Express Entry (New 2026 Categories): IRCC has unveiled "Transport Occupations" and "Senior Managers with Canadian Work Experience" as two of the 10 priority categories for 2026. This allows managers in this NOC to receive invitations with lower CRS scores.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A specialized stream targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents who have integrated into the Canadian workforce.
OINP Employer Job Offer: A reliable route for those with a job offer from a courier or logistics firm in Ontario’s "In-Demand" regions.
BC PNP Skilled Worker: A high-success route for managers overseeing regional or international sorting hubs in British Columbia.
